methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $40M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Nuclear Energy (found 1 same-stage, 1 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 8 same-sector peers exist in Nuclear Energy, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

private.name: Fuse Energy

data.narrative:
Saltfoss Energy is a Series A nuclear energy company founded by Troels Schonfeldt that has raised $40M+. The valuation universe comprises 11 peers: 3 public companies (OKLO, NNE, SMR) and 8 private firms including Fuse Energy, TerraPower, X-Energy, Kairos Power, and Oklo.

Stage-progression methodology was the applicable triangulation approach. Using total capital raised of $40M as a valuation proxy, application of a 2.0x step-up multiplier typical of Series A to subsequent-stage advancement yields an implied valuation of $80M. A peer-median approach could not be applied due to insufficient comparable-stage nuclear energy peers meeting narrow filtering criteria—while 8 same-sector peers exist, fewer than 3 datapoints qualified as same-stage, adjacent-stage, or similar-tier comparables. Relying on an all-sector median would introduce unacceptable methodological bias.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $68M to $92M, with a median point of $80M. This range reflects uncertainty around Stage A capital deployment norms and the emerging nature of the nuclear energy technology segment, where public and private peer valuations exhibit considerable dispersion. Key uncertainties include the absence of a robust narrow peer set, making cross-check validation difficult, and the nascent stage of most comparable companies, limiting historical progression precedents. The $80M midpoint should be treated as a directional estimate pending access to more granular peer financials or revised stage assumptions.
